Class BufferUtils

java.lang.Object
net.fokinatorr.sparkle.util.BufferUtils
public class BufferUtils extends Object
Several utilities for java.nio Buffer API.

    Description
    NonThrowingAutoCloseable
    freeing(Buffer ptr)
    Returns an AutoCloseable that will free the given buffer's memory address in its close() method.
    ByteBuffer
    readFully(InputStream in)
    Reads all bytes from the input stream into a direct ByteBuffer.

    stream(ByteBuffer buffer)
    Creates a stream over the given ByteBuffer without copying its data into the heap.

  • Method Details

    • stream

      public IntStream stream(ByteBuffer buffer)
      Creates a stream over the given ByteBuffer without copying its data into the heap.
      Parameters:
      buffer - the buffer
      Returns:
      a new stream with the given buffer as a source
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if the buffer is null

    • stream

      public IntStream stream(ShortBuffer buffer)
      Creates a stream over the given ShortBuffer without copying its data into the heap.
      Parameters:
      buffer - the buffer
      Returns:
      a new stream with the given buffer as a source
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if the buffer is null

    • stream

      public IntStream stream(CharBuffer buffer)
      Creates a stream over the given CharBuffer without copying its data into the heap.
      Parameters:
      buffer - the buffer
      Returns:
      a new stream with the given buffer as a source
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if the buffer is null

    • stream

      public IntStream stream(IntBuffer buffer)
      Creates a stream over the given IntBuffer without copying its data into the heap.
      Parameters:
      buffer - the buffer
      Returns:
      a new stream with the given buffer as a source
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if the buffer is null

    • stream

      public LongStream stream(LongBuffer buffer)
      Creates a stream over the given LongBuffer without copying its data into the heap.
      Parameters:
      buffer - the buffer
      Returns:
      a new stream with the given buffer as a source
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if the buffer is null

    • stream

      public DoubleStream stream(FloatBuffer buffer)
      Creates a stream over the given FloatBuffer without copying its data into the heap.
      Parameters:
      buffer - the buffer
      Returns:
      a new stream with the given buffer as a source
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if the buffer is null

    • stream

      public DoubleStream stream(DoubleBuffer buffer)
      Creates a stream over the given DoubleBuffer without copying its data into the heap.
      Parameters:
      buffer - the buffer
      Returns:
      a new stream with the given buffer as a source
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if the buffer is null

    • readFully

      public ByteBuffer readFully(InputStream in) throws IOException
      Reads all bytes from the input stream into a direct ByteBuffer. The input stream must be finite, otherwise reading may block forever!

      The buffer must be explicitly freed with MemoryUtil.memFree(Buffer); you can also use freeing(Buffer) to represent the buffer as an AutoCloseable.

      Parameters:
      in - the input stream
      Returns:
      a new direct buffer
      Throws:
      IOException - if an I/O error occurs
      NullPointerException - if the input stream is null

    • freeing

      public NonThrowingAutoCloseable freeing(Buffer ptr)
      Returns an AutoCloseable that will free the given buffer's memory address in its close() method. This method should only be used when the buffer was allocated using MemoryUtil's (n)memAllocXxx(), (n)memCallocXxx() or (n)memReallocXxx() methods.

      A typical usage would be creating an unnamed variable in the try-with-resources statement like this: 

          ByteBuffer bb = MemoryUtil.memAlloc(1024);
    try (var _ = freeing(bb)) {
        // do some stuff with the buffer
    }
      Parameters:
      ptr - the buffer to free
      Returns:
      an AutoCloseable that frees the given buffer
